THE ORDER(S)/JUDGMENT(S) TO BE DELIVERED TO THE COURT NO LATER THAN 9:00 A.M. ON JULY 12, 2024 VIA E-MAIL IN WORD FORMAT. THE ORDERS SHALL CLEARLY IDENTIFY THE IDENTITIES OF THE CURRENT OCCUPANTS OF THE PROPERTY AS WELL AS THEIR STATUS (OWNER, TENANT, ETC). MOVING PARTY IS TO RE-NOTICE THE MOTION. JUDGE: CHARLES F. AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER CONFIRMING REFEREE'S REPORT ON OFFSETS (TRANSACTION ID # 76038925) FILED BY DEFENDANT CARAVANTES, DAVID HEARING SET FOR MAY-16-2025 AT 09:30 AM IN DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER CONFIRMING REFEREE'S REPORT ON OFFSETS DEFENDANT DAVID CARAVANTES AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER CONFIRMING REFEREE'S REPORT ON OFFSETS IS CONTINUED TO MAY 23, 2025 FOR SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING. THE COURT HAS REVIEWED THE PARTIES' SUBMISSIONS MADE IN SUPPORT OF AND IN OPPOSITION TO, THE MOTION. IT ALSO REVIEWED THE REFEREE'S REPORT AND RECOMMENDATION, PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTION FILED MARCH 28, 2025, AND THE REFEREE'S APRIL 29, 2025 DECLARATION FILED IN RESPONSE TO THE OBJECTION. THE COURT FINDS THE REFEREE'S FINDINGS AND RECOMMENDATIONS ARE SUPPORTED BY LAW AND/OR THE EVIDENCE SUBMITTED BY THE PARTIES, WITH THE EXCEPTION OF 2 ISSUES. FIRST, THE COURT DOES NOT AGREE WITH THE AMOUNT AWARDED TO PLAINTIFF IN ATTORNEY'S FEES. THE INVOICES (APPENDIX PAGES 38-62) APPEAR TO SHOW MORE THAN $49,865.20 INCURRED IN FEES. SECOND, IT APPEARS THAT THERE IS AT LEAST SOME EVIDENCE THAT PLAINTIFF MADE ADDITIONAL HELOC PAYMENTS IN THE AMOUNT OF $139.19 ON MARCH 12, 30, AND JUNE 12, 2022. (APPENDIX 816; OBJECTION, 3.) IT'S UNCLEAR WHETHER THE REFEREE CONSIDERED THIS PAYMENT. THE REFEREE SHALL FILE A DECLARATION NO LATER THAN MAY 19, 2025 ADDRESSING BOTH ISSUES. PLAINTIFF MAY FILE A DECLARATION NO LATER THAN MAY 20 AND NO LONGER THAN 4 PAGES, IN RESPONSE TO THE REFEREE'S FINDINGS ON THESE ISSUES. DEFENDANT'S REQUEST FOR ORDER ALLOWING BOTH PARTIES TO CREDIT BID IS DENIED AS PREMATURE. JUDGE: CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: L. YOUNG; NOT REPORTED. (D501)

Off Calendar Sep-08-2025 Continued To Mar-25-26 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Amended Notice Of Motion For An Order Confirming Referee'S Report On Offsets
Off Calendar May-23-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Valerie Ann Valdepena for Plaintiff Mary Ann T. Go (via Zoom).
Matthew L. Taylor, Partition Referee (via Zoom).
Olivia Dajee for Defendant David P. Caravantes (via Zoom).
Diala Debbas for Defendant Third Federal Savings and Loan (via Zoom).

The Court confirmed all parties had reviewed the tentative ruling. Ms. Valdepena objected to the referee's report, asserting that numerous documents-including attorney invoices and financial records-were omitted or disregarded, and that a forensic accounting is warranted due to inconsistencies. Mr. Taylor responded that he had reviewed all submitted documents. Counsel for defendants maintained their positions that the accounting was sufficiently detailed, and the referee's report was supported by the record. Ms. Valdepena reiterated objections regarding missing documentation and inadequate crediting of reimbursable expenses supported by receipts and account statements.

Following discussion and oral argument the court adopted its tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID CARAVANTES' AMENDED NOTICE OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER CONFIRMING REFEREE'S REPORT ON OFFSETS: This matter was continued from May 16 to May 23, 2025 for the Referee to provide a declaration addressing two concerns the Court had with the Referee's Report filed on March 18, 2025. The first dealt with the attorney's fees awarded to Plaintiff, and second with the HELOC payments. The Court provided Plaintiff with an opportunity to respond to the Referee's subsequent findings. The Court having read and considered the Referee's May 19, 2025 declaration and Plaintiff's counsel's response, accepts the Referee's conclusions. The Referee's Report filed on March 18, 2025 shall be amended to recommend a total award of $85,189.23 for the Plaintiff's attorney's fees. The Referee's recommendation on the HELOC loan stands. The Court considered Plaintiff's counsel's declaration which claims that the Referee's accounting still does not take into account all attorney invoices through August 30, 2024. However, a review of the index, (pages 41-62), shows that the Referee did account for all invoices. While counsel's declaration claims she provided all invoices through August 30, 2024, it would appear that is not case. (Valdepena decl., para. 6.) Notably, the appendixes were filed with the court and counsel could have, as the Court did, reviewed the invoices the Referee used in his initial evaluation. Instead, counsel waited until she filed her supplemental declaration to claim that "invoices were provided but seem to be missing." Moreover, she includes none of the missing invoices to her declaration. The Referee is ordered to file an amended report consistent with his amended findings forthwith. The Court finds the Referee's findings and recommendations are supported by law and/or the evidence submitted by the parties. Counsel for the moving party shall provide an order consistent with this tentative ruling and a separate order granting the motion confirming the Referee's amended report. Defendant's request for order allowing both parties to credit bid is denied as premature.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Kenneth B. Hunt; Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT/CROSS COMPLAINT/ANSWER
Off Calendar Jul-25-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

PLAINTIFF MARY ANN GO MOTION TO AMEND COMPLAINT/CROSS COMPLAINT/ANSWER. - DENIED. Plaintiff unreasonably delayed amending the Complaint since she learned of the facts giving rise to the proposed amendments in February and March of 2024, which now cause prejudice to Defendants who moved and obtained interlocutory judgment based on the sole cause of action for partition.

Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC 3.1312(b). Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)

DEFENDANT DAVID CARAVANTES' MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED TRUTH OF FACTS AND GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS AND IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS( TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)

Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DAVID CARAVANTES' MOTION FOR ORDER DEEMING ADMITTED TRUTH OF FACTS AND GENUINENESS OF DOCUMENTS AND IMPOSING MONETARY SANCTIONS( TENTATIVE RULING PART 2 OF 2)

Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Nov-03-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):
Valarie Anne Valdepena, Esq. 909-293-9529 for Plaintiff.
No other Appearance.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

DEFENDANT DAVID CARAVANTES' Motion For Order Deeming Admitted Truth Of Facts And Genuineness Of Documents And Imposing Monetary Sanctions.

RULING - The matter is Uncontested/Discussed. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:The Motion of Defendant David P. Caravantes for Order Deeming Admitted Truth of Facts and Genuineness of Documents and Imposing Monetary Sanctions is denied.

The Court finds that: (1) On June 19, 2023, counsel to Defendant served Requests for Admissions on Plaintiff via email; (2) On July 19, 2023, counsel to Plaintiff timely served verified responses thereto consisting of admissions, denials and objections via email and electronic link; (3) On August 22, 2023, more than a month later, counsel to Defendant claimed via email that the electronic link had failed and requested retransmission; (4) On August 28, 2023, counsel to Plaintiff resent the electronic link via email; (5) There is no evidence that counsel to Defendant responded to the August 28, 2023 email; (6) On October 12, 2023, counsel to Defendant served the instant Motion, making reference to responses received from Plaintiff but asserting that the same were not verified; (6) On October 26, 2023, counsel to Plaintiff emailed counsel to Defendant reminding him of their prior correspondence concerning the responses, enclosing a copy of "her [client's] verifications (sic"), and requesting that the motion be taken off calendar; (7) There is no evidence that counsel to Defendant made any response to the email of October 26, 2023. In addition, Defendant's moving papers admit that a motion deeming matters admitted may not be granted if the Court finds that substantially compliant responses have been served before the hearing of the motion. The Court so finds. The Court further finds that counsel to Defendant's filing and pursuit of this Motion was unwarranted and constitutes an abuse of the discovery process.

Sanctions are awarded against counsel to Defendant and in favor of Plaintiff in the amount of $3000, payable within 20 days after date of Order.

The Court will sign the proposed order submitted if it conforms with the Court's ruling.

Judge Pro Tem: James Fleming, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/jpt)
